items.title: How enterprises are scaling AI
items.summary: OpenAI details enterprise AI scaling from initial experiments to sustained impact, emphasizing trust-building, governance frameworks, workflow integration, and maintaining quality at scale for production deployments.
items.why_it_matters: AI builders gain actionable strategies from OpenAI on overcoming scaling hurdles like governance and workflows, enabling faster enterprise adoption and compounding ROI. Critical for leaders transitioning pilots to enterprise-grade systems with reliability and compliance.
items.suggested_reel_angle: OpenAI reveals enterprise AI scaling secrets: trust, governance, workflows. From experiments to impact!

items.title: OpenAI Campus Network: Student club interest form
items.summary: OpenAI launches Campus Network to unite global student AI clubs, providing exclusive tools, event support, and resources to foster AI-powered campus communities and hands-on projects.
items.why_it_matters: Empowers future AI builders with direct OpenAI access, accelerating talent pipelines through clubs, tools, and events. Builders can engage students early, prototype campus apps, and build ecosystems for innovation.
items.suggested_reel_angle: OpenAI Campus Network: Free AI tools and events for student clubs worldwide!

items.title: GraphDC: A Divide-and-Conquer Multi-Agent System for Scalable Graph Algorithm Reasoning
items.summary: GraphDC introduces a multi-agent divide-and-conquer framework for LLMs to tackle complex graph algorithms, enabling systematic multi-step reasoning on large-scale graphs where single LLMs fail.
items.why_it_matters: Breakthrough for graph reasoning, a LLM weak spot. Builders can implement multi-agent systems for topology-heavy tasks like networks or molecules, scaling to real-world graph sizes with better accuracy.
items.suggested_reel_angle: GraphDC: Multi-agents make LLMs graph reasoning pros on huge datasets!

items.title: MachinaCheck: Building a Multi-Agent CNC Manufacturability System on AMD MI300X
items.summary: Hackathon project deploys multi-agent system on AMD MI300X GPUs for CNC manufacturability checks, integrating AI agents for design validation, simulation, and optimization in hardware workflows.
items.why_it_matters: Proves multi-agent AI viability in manufacturing with high-perf hardware. Builders get blueprint for agentic production tools, leveraging AMD GPUs for real-time checks and bridging AI with physical fabrication.
items.suggested_reel_angle: Multi-agent AI on AMD MI300X revolutionizes CNC manufacturing checks!

items.title: More Thinking, More Bias: Length-Driven Position Bias in Reasoning Models
items.summary: Research shows reasoning models like DeepSeek-R1 exhibit increasing position bias in multiple-choice QA as chain-of-thought length grows, challenging assumptions about longer reasoning reducing heuristics.
items.why_it_matters: Exposes flaw in CoT prompting: more steps amplify biases. Essential for model trainers and prompt engineers to mitigate via debiasing or eval tweaks, improving reliability in reasoning tasks.
items.suggested_reel_angle: Shocking: Longer AI reasoning boosts bias, not fixes it! New arXiv find.
